case_title: ADELE M. ROBERTS VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY PETITIONER ROBERTS, ADELE M. AS TO RESPONDENT RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

LAW AND MOTION, PETITIONER ADELE ROBERTS MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS PER CCP 1094.5. APPEARANCES: REBECCA LIU OF THE OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY (554.3870) FOR RESPONDENT AND OPPOSING PARTY; MICHAEL S. HEBEL OF THE SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS ASSOCIATION (861.0211) FOR PETITIONER AND MOVING PARTY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS DENIED. PETITIONER HAS NOT SHOWN THAT RESPONDENT VIOLATED HOSFORD (1978) 77 CAL.APP.3RD 854. THERE WAS NO SHOWING THAT DR. VON ROGOV MISUNDERSTOOD PETITIONER'S ACTUAL DUTIES AND RESPONDENT REASONABLY RELIED ON THIS DR.'S ASSESSMENT. FURTHER, RESPONDENT'S REASONING IN THE DECISION AND RE-HEARING DECISION ARE COMPELLING. ACCORDINGLY, THE WRIT IS DENIED BECAUSE PETITIONER FAILED TO MEET HER BURDEN UNDER FUKUDA V. CITY OF ANGELS (1999) 20 CAL.4TH 805, 817. ORDER SIGNED IN COURT. THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD PREVIOUSLY LODGED IN THE MATTER WAS RETURNED TO MR. HEBEL. (302)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Mandamus Per Ccp 1094.5
Off Calendar Apr-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER ADELE ROBERTS MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS PER CCP 1094.5.
Appearances: Rebecca Liu of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3870) for respondent and opposing party; Michael S. Hebel of the San Francisco Police Officers Association (861.0211) for petitioner and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion for writ of mandamus is denied. Petitioner has not shown that respondent violated Hosford (1978) 77 Cal.App.3rd 854. There was no showing that Dr. Von Rogov misunderstood petitioner's actual duties and respondent reasonably relied on this Dr.'s assessment. Further, respondent's reasoning in the decision and re-hearing decision are compelling. Accordingly, the writ is denied because petitioner failed to meet her burden under Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 cal.4TH 805, 817. Order signed in court. The administrative record previously lodged in the matter was returned to Mr. Hebel. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
